feed.title: Current Watches, Warnings and Advisories for New Zealand issued by MetService
feed.rights: Copyright Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited, 2026. Licensed under Creative Commons BY 4.0.

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow lowering to 800 metres with accumulations of 1 to 3 cm possible about the summit and lesser amounts down to 800 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Road Snowfall Warning
entry.summary: Snow flurries possible about the summit until 6pm today with accumulations of up to 1 cm. Snow lowering to 800 metres from 6pm today with accumulations of 10 to 15 cm possible about the summit and lesser amounts down to 800 metres.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, amounts may approach warning criteria. Note, heavy rain may continue after midnight Wednesday. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 130 to 180 mm of rain. Peak intensities 15 to 25 mm/h during Tuesday. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 130 to 180 mm of rain. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h during Tuesday. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 200 to 250 mm of rain, but 250 to 350 mm and possibly more about the Inland and Seaward Kaikoura Ranges. During the same period 60 to 100 mm is likely nearer the coast. Peak rates of 15 to 25 mm/h expected Monday night and during Tuesday morning and afternoon, especially about the ranges. Freezing level about 1600 metres and snow likely above 1200 metres. Impact: Dangerous river conditions, flooding, and slips likely. Dangerous driving conditions and road closures are expected. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: High. Changes: Event end time extended. Chance of upgrade increased to high.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Expect 120 to 170 mm of rain, especially about the foothills and high country region, and with 50 to 80 mm over the plains. Note this rain falling as snow above 600 metres. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Moderate. Changes: Upgraded to Orange and area extended.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Warning - Orange
entry.summary: Severe gale southeast winds gusting 130 km/h in about exposed valleys. Impact: Damage to trees, powerlines, and unsecured structures possible. Driving may be difficult, especially for high-sided vehicles and motorcycles. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low. Changes: Upgraded to Orange Warning.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Snow Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy snow above 700 metres, where snowfall amounts may approach warning criteria. Lesser amounts of snow down to 400 metres. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate. Changes: Event timing changed.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Strong Wind Watch
entry.summary: Southeast winds may approach severe gale in exposed places. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: A further 20 to 40 mm of rain on top of what has already fallen. Intensities have generally decreased, but max rates of 5 to 7 mm/h are expected. Snow is likely above 400 metres. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low. Changes: Warning extended to 8pm.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Warning - Orange
entry.summary: A further 15 to 25 mm of rain on top of what has already fallen. Intensities have generally decreased, but max rates of 5 to 7 mm/h are expected. Snow is likely above 400 metres. Impact: Streams and rivers may rise rapidly. Surface flooding, slips, and difficult driving conditions possible. Chance of upgrading to a Red Warning: Low. Changes: Rain Warning has been lifted for Clutha and Dunedin about and south of the City.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited

entry.title: Heavy Rain Watch
entry.summary: Periods of heavy rain, and amounts may approach warning criteria. Chance of upgrading to a Warning: Moderate.

author.name: Meteorological Service of New Zealand Limited
